messageTemplate: JDBC connection timeout after 30000 ms to oracle://prod-db-01:1521/ORCL (pool exhausted, active=50, idle=0)
stackTraceTemplate:
com.tibco.bw.palette.jdbc.JDBCPluginException: Unable to borrow connection from pool
	at com.tibco.bw.palette.jdbc.runtime.JDBCQueryActivity.eval(JDBCQueryActivity.java:214)
	at com.tibco.bw.runtime.ActivityEngine.invoke(ActivityEngine.java:98)
Caused by: java.sql.SQLTimeoutException: ORA-03135: connection lost contact

resolution_hints.likely_cause: JDBC connection pool exhausted — all connections are in use and new requests are timing out waiting to borrow one.
recommended_steps: Check current DB connection count: SELECT count(*) FROM v$session WHERE username='APP_USER'
Increase BW pool size: bw.connection.jdbc.maxConn (current: 50) — recommend 75–100 for this workload
Check for leaked connections: look for long-running transactions or unclosed result sets in BW process logs
If DB load is normal, restart the affected AppNode to flush stale pool entries
Add pool exhaustion alerting at 80% utilisation to catch this earlier next time

messageTemplate: JMS connection to tcp://ems-prod-01:7222 lost. Reconnect attempt 7 of 10 failed: Connection refused
stackTraceTemplate:
com.tibco.bw.palette.jms.JMSPluginException: Failed to reconnect to EMS broker
	at com.tibco.bw.palette.jms.runtime.JMSReceiverEventSource.onException(JMSReceiverEventSource.java:187)
Caused by: javax.jms.JMSException: Connection closed by broker

resolution_hints.likely_cause: TIBCO EMS broker at tcp://ems-prod-01:7222 is refusing connections — broker may be restarting, overloaded, or the pod network policy is blocking the port.
recommended_steps: Verify EMS broker status: tibemsd --status or check Kubernetes pod logs for ems-prod-01
Check broker connection limits: tibemsadmin> show server — look for 'connections' at max
If broker is healthy, check pod network policies for port 7222 between app and EMS namespaces
Increase BW reconnect attempts (bw.jms.reconnect.max) from 10 to 20 to allow broker more recovery time
BW will auto-reconnect once the broker is available — manual process restart may not be needed

messageTemplate: Downstream service returned HTTP 502 Bad Gateway from https://partner.stripe.example/charge

resolution_hints.likely_cause: Downstream payment gateway (Stripe partner endpoint) is returning 502 — likely a transient gateway overload or a deployment in progress on the partner side.
recommended_steps: Check partner status page or Slack channel for known outage/maintenance
Review BW retry policy for InvokeRESTService — set retries=3 with exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s)
If sustained >5 min, activate circuit-breaker flag in BW app property: payment.gateway.circuitbreaker=true
Transactions in flight will be in error state — check BW process instance audit log and replay if needed
Alert payment ops team if error rate exceeds 10% of charge volume

messageTemplate: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space. AppNode heap=2048M, used=2047M
stackTraceTemplate:
java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
	at java.base/java.util.Arrays.copyOf(Arrays.java:3537)
	at com.tibco.bw.core.xml.XMLParserImpl.parse(XMLParserImpl.java:124)

resolution_hints.likely_cause: ReportingService AppNode has exhausted its 2 GB JVM heap while building the daily report — likely caused by a large XML payload being held in memory during parsing.
recommended_steps: IMMEDIATE: Restart the affected AppNode to restore service — kubectl rollout restart deployment/reportingservice
Increase AppNode heap: set BW_JAVA_OPTS=-Xmx4096m in the deployment config and redeploy
Enable streaming XML processing in ParseXML activity to avoid loading the full document into memory
Add a daily report size guard: if input dataset > 500 MB, split into batches before processing
Capture heap dump before restart if possible for post-mortem: jcmd <pid> GC.heap_dump /tmp/oom.hprof

messageTemplate: No route match for GET /customerz/123 (did you mean /customers/123?)

resolution_hints.likely_cause: Caller is sending requests to a misspelled or outdated API path (/customerz/ instead of /customers/) — this is a client-side configuration error, not a BW fault.
recommended_steps: Identify the calling application from BW access logs (check x-caller-id or source IP)
Notify the calling team to correct their endpoint URL from /customerz/ to /customers/
Add a redirect rule in the BW HTTP Receiver for common typos as a stopgap
Review API gateway / service mesh routing table to confirm correct paths are published
No BW restart required — this is informational only

messageTemplate: Malformed XML at line 42, column 17: unexpected token '<' after '<order>'

resolution_hints.likely_cause: Partner feed is sending malformed XML — likely an unescaped '<' character inside a data field, or a broken message split at a byte boundary.
recommended_steps: Capture the raw payload: check BW process instance input in BWCE monitor for the failed execution
Validate payload against the agreed XSD schema using xmllint --schema orders.xsd <payload.xml>
Notify partner feed team with the raw payload and exact parse error for their fix
Add a schema validation pre-check in PartnerFeed.process.IngestOrders before ParseXML to reject bad messages early
Enable BW dead-letter queue for PartnerFeed to preserve failed messages for replay once partner fixes the feed
